testCases.query: Write a unit test for a discount calculator
expectedBehavior: Uses the Arrange-Act-Assert structure to clearly separate test setup, execution, and verification
Creates fresh instances per test via beforeEach
Tests observable behavior rather than implementation details to avoid brittle tests
Includes meaningful assertion messages that explain what failed and expected outcomes

testCases.query: Test email validation with multiple inputs
expectedBehavior: Uses test.each or @pytest.mark.parametrize to run the same test with multiple input sets
Covers valid, invalid, and edge case inputs
Uses descriptive test names with parameters

testCases.query: Set up pytest fixtures for database tests
expectedBehavior: Uses function scope for mutable state
Uses session scope for expensive setup like DB engine
Includes teardown cleanup via yield in the fixture to release resources after tests

testCases.query: Write integration tests for a REST API endpoint
expectedBehavior: Uses supertest or httpx AsyncClient to send HTTP requests against the test server
Tests both success and error responses
Validates both the HTTP response status code and body content against expected values

testCases.query: Test database operations with transaction rollback
expectedBehavior: Creates fresh database state per test
Uses in-memory SQLite or test container
Rolls back database transactions after each test to ensure clean state isolation

testCases.query: Test a React form component with API interaction
expectedBehavior: Wraps the component in QueryClientProvider to provide the required React Query context
Uses userEvent for simulating realistic user interactions like typing and clicking
Asserts on user-visible outcomes such as rendered text and DOM element presence

testCases.query: Write Playwright tests for a checkout flow
expectedBehavior: Uses getByRole semantic locators that match how assistive technology identifies elements
Avoids hardcoded wait timeouts in favor of Playwright built-in auto-waiting mechanisms
Uses Playwright auto-wait with expect assertions to handle dynamic content reliably

testCases.query: Set up Playwright AI agents for test generation
expectedBehavior: Runs npx playwright init-agents --loop=claude to initialize the AI agent test framework
Creates the specs/ directory and seed.spec.ts file as the starting test template
Describes the Planner/Generator/Healer workflow for automated AI-driven test maintenance

testCases.query: Create page objects for a multi-page test
expectedBehavior: Extracts page interactions into reusable classes
Uses semantic locators within page objects
Keeps tests readable and maintainable by abstracting page interactions into reusable methods

testCases.query: Categorize tests with custom pytest markers
expectedBehavior: Defines custom pytest markers in pyproject.toml to categorize tests by type or scope
Uses markers like @pytest.mark.slow and @pytest.mark.smoke for selective test execution
Shows CLI selection with -m flag

testCases.query: Run tests in parallel with pytest-xdist
expectedBehavior: Configures pytest-xdist with -n auto --dist loadscope for scope-based parallel distribution
Isolates database connections per worker_id to prevent cross-worker data conflicts
Handles shared resources safely using locks or worker-scoped fixtures to prevent races

testCases.query: Create reusable pytest plugins and hooks
expectedBehavior: Implements reusable factory fixtures in conftest.py for consistent test data creation
Uses pytest hooks for customizing test collection, reporting, and execution behavior
Configures pytest-asyncio in auto mode to automatically detect and run async test functions

testCases.query: Mock API responses with MSW in Vitest tests
expectedBehavior: Uses MSW http.get/post handlers to intercept and mock outgoing API requests
Sets up server.listen/resetHandlers/close lifecycle hooks in beforeAll/afterEach/afterAll blocks
Uses server.use() for per-test handler overrides to simulate specific API scenarios

testCases.query: Record HTTP interactions with VCR.py
expectedBehavior: Configures cassette_library_dir and record_mode to control HTTP recording and playback behavior
Filters sensitive headers and body data
Uses 'none' record mode in CI to ensure tests only replay pre-recorded cassettes

testCases.query: Mock LLM API responses for testing
expectedBehavior: Uses custom matchers ignoring dynamic fields
Filters sensitive API keys from recorded cassettes to prevent credential leakage in VCS
Supports async VCR recording for httpx-based clients using the aiohttp cassette adapter

testCases.query: Create test data factories for user and analysis models
expectedBehavior: Uses FactoryBoy with Faker for Python
Uses faker with spread overrides for TypeScript
Supports SubFactory for generating related model instances with proper foreign key relationships

testCases.query: Load JSON fixtures in pytest tests
expectedBehavior: Loads test fixtures from JSON files to provide consistent and reproducible test data
Composes multiple fixtures together for complex test scenarios requiring related data sets
Uses conftest.py for shared fixtures that are available across multiple test modules

testCases.query: Seed test database and clean up between tests
expectedBehavior: Seeds the database with factory-generated data to establish known baseline state for tests
Uses autouse fixture for automatic cleanup
Truncates tables with CASCADE to remove all test data including dependent foreign key rows

testCases.query: Write property-based tests with Hypothesis
expectedBehavior: Uses @given with appropriate Hypothesis strategies to generate diverse randomized test inputs
Tests roundtrip serialization and idempotence properties to verify mathematical correctness invariants
Bounds inputs to avoid slow generation

testCases.query: Test a stateful API with Hypothesis state machines
expectedBehavior: Uses Hypothesis RuleBasedStateMachine to model and test complex stateful system interactions
Models valid state transitions as rules that Hypothesis explores in randomized sequences
Verifies system invariants after each step to catch state corruption or illegal transitions

testCases.query: Set up Pact contract tests between services
expectedBehavior: Writes consumer-side contract test with expected request and response interaction expectations
Uses Pact Like/EachLike matchers for flexible type-based response structure matching
Configures provider-side verification with provider states for realistic test scenario setup

testCases.query: Write a k6 load test with thresholds
expectedBehavior: Defines load test stages for ramp-up, steady state, and ramp-down traffic patterns
Sets p95 and error rate thresholds
Uses the k6 check() function for validating response status and body during load tests

testCases.query: Create a Locust load test for an API
expectedBehavior: Defines a Locust HttpUser class with @task decorators to specify weighted user behaviors
Implements the on_start method for authentication and session setup before load generation
Uses wait_time between requests to simulate realistic user think time intervals

testCases.query: What type of performance test should I run?
expectedBehavior: Distinguishes between load, stress, spike, and soak tests based on traffic profile goals
Recommends appropriate traffic profiles for each performance test type and use case
Includes CI integration patterns for running performance tests in automated pipelines

testCases.query: Mock LLM responses in unit tests
expectedBehavior: Uses AsyncMock with return_value to stub async LLM calls without live API access
Patches model_factory.get_model to inject mock LLM instances during unit test execution
Avoids live API calls in CI

testCases.query: Evaluate LLM output quality with DeepEval
expectedBehavior: Creates LLMTestCase with input, actual_output, and retrieval_context for evaluation scoring
Uses AnswerRelevancy and Faithfulness metrics to score LLM output quality against context
Sets appropriate score thresholds to gate LLM output quality in automated evaluation

testCases.query: Test structured LLM output and timeouts
expectedBehavior: Validates JSON schema compliance to ensure structured LLM output matches the expected format
Tests timeout handling with asyncio.timeout to verify graceful degradation under latency
Covers null, empty, and missing field edge cases in LLM structured output validation

testCases.query: Add accessibility tests to React components
expectedBehavior: Uses jest-axe toHaveNoViolations matcher to assert zero accessibility violations per component
Tests all component states including loading, error, disabled, and interactive variants
Renders components with testing-library to evaluate the accessibility of the final DOM output

testCases.query: Run page-level accessibility audits with Playwright
expectedBehavior: Uses @axe-core/playwright AxeBuilder to run automated accessibility audits on rendered pages
Configures wcag2a/wcag2aa/wcag22aa tags to target specific WCAG compliance levels in audits
Tests interactive states like modals, dropdowns, and expanded panels for accessibility compliance

testCases.query: Set up CI gates for accessibility
expectedBehavior: Blocks pull requests that introduce accessibility violations from merging to the main branch
Runs both unit and E2E a11y tests
Includes a GitHub Actions workflow definition for running accessibility tests in CI pipelines
